data.title: U.S. Senate Agriculture Committee Delays Crypto Market Structure Bill Revision
data.description: According to PANews, U.S. Senate Agriculture Committee Chairman John Boozman announced a delay in the committee's revision of the cryptocurrency market structure bill. Initially scheduled to coincide with the Senate Banking Committee's session on Thursday, the revision will now take place in the last week of January. Boozman stated that additional time is necessary to maintain bipartisan support.

data.title: Markup of Senate Crypto Market Structure Bill Pushed to Late January
data.description:
Senate leaders have delayed a key vote on crypto market structure legislation, citing unresolved policy issues around stablecoin yield.
source: https://decrypt.co/354357/markup-senate-crypto-market-structure-bill-pushed-late-january

data.title: U.S. Economic Growth Forecasted to Reach 2.5% to 2.75% by 2026
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve official Williams has indicated that the United States' economic growth rate could range between 2.5% and 2.75% by 2026.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams Optimistic About 2026 Economic Outlook
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve's Williams has expressed optimism about the economic outlook for 2026, describing it as "quite favorable." He also noted that current monetary policy is closer to a neutral stance.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams Emphasizes Inflation Control Without Job Market Risks
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve's Williams stated that the Federal Reserve must reduce inflation without posing undue risks to the job market.

data.title: Meta Platforms Considers Layoffs in Reality Labs Division
data.description: According to Odaily, Meta Platforms is contemplating layoffs within its Reality Labs division, specifically targeting the branch involved in 'metaverse' operations. Three informed employees revealed that the company might initiate these layoffs as early as next month, potentially affecting 10% to 30% of the team. This team primarily focuses on VR headsets and VR-based social networks. Reality Labs comprises the metaverse and wearable devices departments. Sources indicate that management plans to redirect the savings from these layoffs towards the AR glasses project. Meta launched AR glasses in collaboration with Ray-Ban in 2021, surpassing internal sales targets. Insiders report that Meta is reassessing its investments in the metaverse, with executives considering significant cuts to VR-related positions within the metaverse department.

data.title: BlackRock Chief Investment Officer Rick Rieder believes the Federal Reserve seats are independent.
data.description: Rick Rieder, chief investment officer at BlackRock and a potential candidate for Federal Reserve chair, believes the Fed seat is independent.

data.title: Meta plans to cut investment in its Metaverse team and redirect funds to its VR glasses business.
data.description: According to The New York Times, three employees familiar with the matter said that Meta Platforms (META.O) is considering layoffs in a branch of its Reality Labs division that works on the "metaverse" business, potentially starting as early as next month, affecting 10% to 30% of the team's employees. This team is primarily responsible for VR headsets and VR-based social networks. Reality Labs comprises the Metaverse and Wearables divisions. Sources indicate that management expects to redirect the savings from the layoffs to an AR glasses project. Meta launched AR glasses in 2021 in partnership with Ray-Ban, and sales have exceeded internal targets. The sources say that Meta is reassessing its metaverse investments, and within the metaverse division, executives are considering significant cuts to VR-related positions.

data.title: White House: Diplomacy is the U.S.'s "preferred" solution to the situation in Iran.
data.description: White House Press Secretary Janet Levitt said in a media interview on the 12th that, facing the current situation in Iran, US President Trump considers diplomacy the "preferred" option. Levitt said Trump "has consistently emphasized that diplomacy is the preferred option," but if he deems it necessary, he "will not hesitate to use the US military." Trump said on the 11th that Iran had contacted US government officials and proposed negotiations, and that "meetings are being arranged." Meanwhile, the US is considering a range of measures to address the situation in Iran, including "some very tough options" such as military action. Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi stated at a meeting of foreign diplomats in Tehran on the 12th that Iran is prepared to negotiate with the US on the basis of "mutual respect," but is also prepared for war.

data.title: Wall Street Journal: The White House weighs its Iran nuclear proposal, advising Trump to "try diplomacy first, then force."
data.description: According to the Wall Street Journal, U.S. officials say the White House is weighing Iran's final diplomatic engagement proposal to limit its nuclear program, even as Trump currently leans toward authorizing a new military strike against Iran. Officials say some senior government aides, led by Vice President Vance, are urging Trump to try diplomatic means before “retaliating” against Iran’s actions in the unrest.
